case_id: Anita Roy / EMP-9001 (target); Rajiv Menon / EMP-9002 (decoy)

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 20 and 24 create EMPLOYEE row with FIRST_NAME='Anita', LAST_NAME='Roy', EMP_CODE='EMP-9001'; confirmed in task description line 5
setup_task.sh line 41 creates LEAVE_APPLICATION with FROM_DATE='2025-10-15' for EMP-9001; matches task description

setup_task.sh line 41 assigns LEAVE_TYPE_ID=2 to Anita Roy's leave. Line 39 contains explicit comment: 'Assuming Leave Type 2 is Sick Leave, 1 is Casual, 3 is Privilege.' This is marked as an assumption, not a verified fact. No database schema documentation available to confirm actual type ID mapping.
setup_task.sh line 27 and 30 create EMPLOYEE row with FIRST_NAME='Rajiv', LAST_NAME='Menon', EMP_CODE='EMP-9002'; confirmed in task description line 5
setup_task.sh line 47 creates LEAVE_APPLICATION with FROM_DATE='2025-10-20' for EMP-9002; matches task description

Multiple validated_pi.json files (configure_week_off, manual_attendance_entry, perform_bulk_salary_revision) confirm actual database is DEMO.FDB not ABORADATA.FDB. Path verified via setup_attendhrm.ps1 line 370-371 which searches for 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Attend HRM\Data\DEMO.FDB'. Evidence: setup_task.sh lines 63-65 search for *.fdb in Lenvica paths but the actual system uses Attend HRM paths per environment documentation. The claimed ABORADATA.FDB file does not exist in the environment.
Same as db_path_hints[0] - verified via environment documentation and setup scripts. Actual path uses 'Attend HRM' directory and 'DEMO.FDB' filename.
Same as db_path_hints[0-1] - ProgramData location is not used for AttendHRM database in this environment. The actual database is installed with the application in Program Files.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires an agent to approve a pending Sick Leave request for employee Anita Roy (EMP-9001) dated October 15, 2025 in the AttendHRM HR management system. The setup script creates two synthetic employee records: Anita Roy with a pending leave application (assigned LEAVE_TYPE_ID=2, assumed to be 'Sick Leave' but unverified) and Rajiv Menon with a decoy pending leave application dated October 20, 2025 (assigned LEAVE_TYPE_ID=3). The task uses Firebird SQL database with employee and leave application records created fresh via SQL MERGE/INSERT statements. Critical discrepancy: All three db_path_hints claim the database is at various Lenvica/ABORADATA.FDB locations, but the actual AttendHRM environment uses DEMO.FDB located at C:\Program Files (x86)\Attend HRM\Data\DEMO.FDB, as confirmed by multiple verified_pi.json files and setup scripts.
